leagueBriefing: Ranked powerhouses like Arkansas and Virginia survived scares to keep their winning streaks alive, while USC needed a dramatic walk-off to avoid an upset. The midweek slate was defined by high-scoring rallies and tight pitching duels across the country.

games.headline: Lee dominates early as Razorbacks sweep midweek series
games.brief: Peyton Lee was the story in Fayetteville, carrying a no-hitter into the fifth inning to help the Razorbacks finish off a two-game sweep of Northern Colorado. Arkansas jumped out to a 9-0 lead by the seventh before the Bears finally found some life against the bullpen, but the cushion was more than enough to secure the Hogs' 16th win of the season.
bullets: Freshman Peyton Lee struck out six and allowed just one hit over five scoreless innings.
Arkansas improved to 5-1 in midweek games this season.
Damian Ruiz reached base four times and drove in two runs in his first start in center field.
The Razorbacks recorded four stolen bases, bringing their season total to 33 through 22 games.
games.context: The Razorbacks carry momentum into their first road test of the season, a weekend series at South Carolina.

games.headline: Virginia holds off furious Liberty rally in high-scoring thriller
games.brief: What looked like a blowout turned into a nail-biter as Virginia survived an eight-run surge from Liberty over the final three innings. The Cavaliers built a massive 14-4 lead behind a seven-run first inning, but the Flames brought the go-ahead run to the plate in the ninth before Lucas Hartman finally slammed the door to preserve the road win.
bullets: Virginia exploded for seven runs in the first inning on just one hit, aided by three walks and a hit batsman.
Liberty outhit Virginia 17-9 but left 13 men on base.
Flames first baseman Riley DeCandido homered twice and drove in four runs in the comeback attempt.
Jake Weatherspoon led the Cavaliers' offense with four RBIs.
games.context: The win marks Virginia's 18th victory as they continue to solidify their spot in the top 10.

games.headline: Basseer's walk-off blast rescues Trojans in ninth
games.brief: Trailing for the entire game, No. 13 USC found magic in the bottom of the ninth when Jack Basseer launched a 409-foot two-run homer to left. The Trojans' offense had been stifled by the Roadrunners for eight innings, but the walk-off victory pushed their incredible season record to 21-1.
bullets: Jack Basseer hit his team-leading fifth home run of the year to win it.
The Trojans were held to just four hits all night by Bakersfield pitching.
Henry Chabot earned the win with two scoreless innings of hitless relief.
Cal State Bakersfield led 3-0 through the fifth inning before USC began its comeback.
games.context: USC continues one of the hottest starts in program history as they head back into Big Ten play.

games.headline: Tar Heels use late five-run surge to pull away from Spartans
games.brief: North Carolina found itself in a deadlocked 2-2 battle through six innings before the bats finally woke up. A massive five-run seventh inning, highlighted by a Rom Kellis home run, allowed the Tar Heels to breathe easy and secure a midweek win over a pesky UNCG squad.
bullets: The game was tied at 2-2 until the sixth inning when UNC regained the lead.
UNC utilized seven different pitchers in a scheduled staff day.
Luke Jenkins drove in both runs for the Spartans with a two-out single in the fourth.
North Carolina outhit the Spartans 9-5 and left 15 runners on base.
games.context: The win keeps North Carolina steady as they prepare for a high-stakes ACC schedule.

games.headline: Murphy's walk-off single knocks off No. 16 Coastal Carolina
games.brief: Campbell secured its highest-ranked win in two years in dramatic fashion on Wednesday night. After Coastal Carolina tied the game with a sacrifice fly in the top of the ninth, Mike Murphy delivered a base hit up the middle in the bottom half to give the Camels a signature victory over the 16th-ranked Chanticleers.
bullets: Campbell pitchers allowed only one hit the entire game while striking out 14 batters.
The Camels issued 10 walks, which allowed Coastal to stay in the game despite the lack of hits.
This was Campbell's first win over a ranked opponent since February 2025.
Mike Murphy's game-winner was the first hit for either team with runners in scoring position (combined 1-for-24).
games.context: This upset loss snaps a winning streak for Coastal Carolina and boosts Campbell's resume heading into conference play.

games.headline: Lichtenberger drives in five as Clemson cruises in Charleston
games.brief: In front of a record-setting crowd at Riley Park, Clemson showed why they are a top-20 mainstay. Tyler Lichtenberger led the charge with a five-RBI performance, as the Tigers used a strong middle-inning push to silence the Bulldogs and complete their sweep of the Lowcountry road trip.
bullets: Tyler Lichtenberger went 3-for-5 with five RBIs and two runs scored.
The game featured a record-setting attendance of 5,298 fans.
Clemson scored all nine of their runs in the fourth, fifth, and sixth innings.
The Citadel’s Andrew Bowers took the loss after giving up seven hits in 3.2 innings.
games.context: The Tigers remain one of the most consistent teams in the ACC, improving to 18-4.

games.headline: Ragin' Cajuns explode in 10th to outlast Houston
games.brief: Louisiana and Houston traded blows for nine innings before the Ragin' Cajuns finally broke through in extras. Capitalizing on a critical Houston throwing error and a wild pitch, Louisiana plated three runs in the top of the 10th to secure a hard-fought midweek victory on the road.
bullets: Colt Brown homered in the fourth and added a key RBI groundout in the 10th.
Louisiana's Rigoberto Hernandez reached on a fielder's choice in the 10th that sparked the three-run rally.
Cody Brasch earned the win in relief, while Houston's Kendall Hoffman took the loss.
Houston's Riley Jackson hit his fourth home run of the season in the second inning.
games.context: Louisiana earns a high-quality midweek road win against a Big 12 opponent.
